text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The goal is not to add new features, but to make the code cleaner, easier to read, and simpler to maintain. For example, you might rename confusing variables, split a long function into smaller parts, or remove duplicated logic. Refactoring helps reduce technical debt, which is the cost of fixing messy or inefficient code later. By regularly refactoring, developers keep projects manageable and avoid problems that slow down future work. In short, well-structured code is easier to understand and modify, saving time and effort in the long run.
